title: tagetes hartwegii
intro: Tagetes hartwegii, also known as Mexican Marigold, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Tagetes hartwegii is believed to have various health benefits, including its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infused oils for topical or internal use, making it a versatile addition to natural medicine practices.
